message:
chore(deps): bump asupersync 0.2.9 → 0.3.1 with patch-rev catch-up

Bumps the workspace-level `asupersync` and `asupersync-macros` deps
from 0.2.9 → 0.3.1 to pick up today's crates.io release (published
2026-04-21 23:58 UTC) and keeps the `[patch.crates-io]` / per-upstream-
repo patches single-sourced on the matching v0.3.1 tag SHA.

## What changed at a glance

* `Cargo.toml` — the four `[patch]` sections that pin `asupersync` to
  a git rev now point to `0f04de1c709064d5e0f7e8721f669f66f787e807`
  (tag `v0.3.1` upstream) instead of `6be1c989`. Workspace version
  strings for `asupersync` and `asupersync-macros` move to `"0.3.1"`.

* `Cargo.lock` — cargo update resolves the new `asupersync` /
  `asupersync-macros` / `franken-decision` / `franken-evidence` /
  `franken-kernel` all at 0.3.1 (both the registry entries from
  crates.io and the git-rev entries from the patched repo) and pulls
  in the transitive additions the 0.3.x release carried:
  `hashbrown 0.17.0`, `hmac 0.13.0`, `hybrid-array 0.4.10`, `sha1
  0.11.0`, `sha2 0.11.0`, `digest 0.11.2`, `block-buffer 0.12.0`,
  `crypto-common 0.2.1`, `const-oid 0.10.2`, `cmov 0.5.3`, `ctutils
  0.4.2`. No crate is downgraded; nothing drops out of the graph.

## Why patches stay

The `[patch]` sections remain because fastmcp_rust, fastapi_rust, and
frankensearch continue to reference `asupersync` via relative path
deps pointing outside their own repos (`../asupersync`). Without the
patch entries, cargo cannot resolve those transitive path deps when
those upstream repos are fetched as git dependencies. The comment at
lines 554-557 documents that constraint; only the revs change here.

## Source-code migration (outcome.rs)

The 0.2 → 0.3 boundary of `asupersync::CancelReason` changed the
`message` field from `Option<&'static str>` to `Option<String>`. The
three `cancel_user` / `cancel_timeout` / `cancel_shutdown` helpers in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/outcome.rs` now wrap their `&'static str`
argument with `.to_string()` before storing it, preserving the public
helper signatures (`fn cancel_*(message: &'static str) -> CancelReason`)
so every call site continues to compile unchanged. This is the only
source-code touch the bump required.

## Verification

* `cargo update -p asupersync -p asupersync-macros` — clean resolve
  with 5 crate bumps (the four `franken-*` / `asupersync-macros`
  siblings that travel together) and the transitive hash/crypto
  additions listed above.
* `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features`
  (isolated target dir `/tmp/ft-asupersync-check`) — clean build, no
  new errors vs the pre-bump state.

Other compile errors visible in `cargo check --features asupersync-
runtime` (`runtime.rs:3480` `exit_reason` scope, plus the workspace
cargo-check gaps tracked by ft-h9g0q / ft-h8u5y) are pre-existing on
the current tree and are being worked by separate beads; this commit
does not touch them.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
